steps.title: Identify the exponential form
steps.content: The ball starts at $24$ feet, so the initial value is $24$. After each bounce, the height is $80\%$ of the previous height, so the multiplier is $0.80$.

steps.title: Write the function
steps.content: After $n$ bounces, the initial height is multiplied by $0.80$ a total of $n$ times: $$h(n)=24(0.80)^n.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: $h(n)=24{(0.24)}^{n}$ uses $0.24$ as the multiplier. $h(n)=80{ \left(0.24\right)}^{n}$ uses the wrong initial height and wrong multiplier. $h(n)=80{(0.80)}^{n}$ uses the wrong initial height. The model $h(n)=24{(0.80)}^{n}$ has initial value $24$ and multiplier $0.80$, so the correct answer is choice B.
